text.title: Product Operations Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed across deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: PM spends 40% of week maintaining the experiment platform; discovery time dies.
antipattern.root-cause: No Product Ops function; tooling defaulted to whoever set it up.
antipattern.fix: Assign Product Ops as owner of roadmap tool, experiment platform, feedback ingest, metric warehouse; PMs off the on-call.

antipattern.symptom: Three different weekly metric rollups circulate; stakeholders ask which number is real.
antipattern.root-cause: Multiple teams produced their own rollups; no canonical owner.
antipattern.fix: Designate one canonical weekly rollup; kill or merge duplicates.

antipattern.symptom: Stakeholders re-learn the update shape each week; engagement drops.
antipattern.root-cause: No fixed template; each PM ships their own.
antipattern.fix: Mandate fixed template (north-star, delta, top-3 wins, top-3 risks, asks); enforce in CI.

antipattern.symptom: Ops headcount doubles in a year; processes still manual.
antipattern.root-cause: Default response to new pain is hiring, not automating.
antipattern.fix: Require an automation attempt + result before any Ops headcount request.
